title: Molten Italian Monterey Jack Cheese Lasagna
description: A savory and indulgent Italian dish featuring molten Monterey Jack cheese, served in a dolsot stone bowl. This lasagna is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to package instructions.
In a large skillet, combine the ground beef and Italian seasoning, and cook until the beef is browned.
In a large bowl, combine the ricotta cheese, mozzarella cheese, and parmesan cheese.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the marinara sauce and a dash of salt and pepper.
In a dolsot stone bowl, create a thin layer of the marinara sauce.
Arrange 4 lasagna noodles on top of the sauce.
Spread half of the ricotta cheese mixture over the noodles.
Sprinkle with half of the Monterey Jack cheese.
Repeat the layers, starting with the marinara sauce, then the noodles, the ricotta cheese mixture, and finally the remaining Monterey Jack cheese.
Cover the bowl with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
